rationale: X's core analysis of orchestrator.py:1290 mirrors yJader's actual reasoning almost exactly (both submit paths reject lists, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was the bug) and correctly reaches APPROVE, matching the two LGTM reviews. Y raises the same line but hedges with repetitive, contradictory 'confirm this works' comments (major/minor duplicates) that never resolve to the actual insight, then unjustifiably lands on REQUEST CHANGES despite the fix being verifiably correct and human-approved, hurting precision and trustworthiness. Both are grounded in the diff and reasonably locate the right lines, but X is more correct, concise, and better calibrated to the ground truth verdict.
